title: Smoky Grilled Malaysian Pork Satay
description: A fresh and springy Malaysian dish featuring pork, cider, and a blend of spices, grilled to perfection on a binchotan grill, suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering.
instructions: Prepare the binchotan grill by heating it to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Thread the pork strips onto the bamboo skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
In a large bowl, whisk together the cider, coconut milk, coriander, cumin, turmeric, salt, and pepper to make the marinade.
Add the pork skewers to the marinade and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the pork skewers from the marinade and brush with vegetable oil.
Grill the pork skewers for 5-7 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
Serve the grilled pork satay hot with a side of peanut sauce or kecap manis.
Garnish with sliced cucumbers, onions, and cilantro for added freshness and flavor.
